definitions.definition: The process of changing the existing partnership agreement between partners due to certain events such as admission of new partners, retirement of partners, death of partners, etc. It involves the revaluation of assets and liabilities of the firm.
The process of bringing a new partner into the existing partnership, which involves the revaluation of the assets and liabilities of the firm.
A person who joins the existing partnership by bringing in his share of capital and becomes a partner in the firm.
Another term for the new partner who joins the existing partnership.
A partner who retires or leaves the partnership.
The excess of the amount for which the business is sold over the net value of its assets.
The process of revaluing the assets and liabilities of the firm at the time of admission of a new partner, which involves determining their current values.
The amount set aside by the partners to meet any future losses or to provide for the payment of a partner's share on retirement.
The ratio of the amount of goodwill sacrificed by the outgoing partner to the total goodwill.
The outgoing partner who sacrifices a part of the goodwill.
The value of goodwill calculated by multiplying the capital of the new partner by the sacrifice ratio.
The value of goodwill sacrificed by the outgoing partner.

definitions.term: New Value of the Firm's Assets
definitions.definition: The total value of the assets of the firm after revaluation.

definitions.term: Old Value of the Firm's Assets
definitions.definition: The total value of the assets of the firm before revaluation.
The difference between the new value of the firm's assets and its liabilities.
Journal entries made to record the revaluation of assets and liabilities of the firm.
The accounting treatment of goodwill when it is admitted into the partnership.
